name: HC-SR04
tagline: Measures distance (2cm to 400cm) using ultrasonic sound waves
description: The HC-SR04 is a popular, low-cost ultrasonic distance sensor. It works by sending out an ultrasonic burst (40kHz sound waves) from the transmitter trigger pin, and measuring the time it takes for the echo pulse to bounce back and be received by the receiver pin. This time-of-flight value is then converted into a distance reading. Ideal for obstacle detection, robotics, and water level monitoring.

pinout.name: Echo
pinout.desc: Receive echo return pulse (digital input pin)

wiringNote: Connect Trig pin to Arduino D2 and Echo pin to D3. The sensor operates strictly at 5V, so ensure a proper 5V power supply from the microcontroller.
libraryName: No library required (can use PulseIn()), or HCSR04/NewPing
libraryInstall: Standard pulseIn() is built-in. Optionally search for 'NewPing' in Arduino Library Manager for optimized multi-sensor usage.
commonMistakes: Connecting VCC to 3.3V instead of 5V → incorrect or zero distance readings
Not waiting at least 60ms between measurements to avoid echo interference
